title: Fix 'Failed to fetch models' error when adding a provider
prompt:
When adding a new provider (in the per-agent Providers settings / provider dialog), clicking to fetch the available models fails with:

    Failed to fetch models: Was there a typo in the url or port?

Investigate and fix the model-discovery / fetch-models code path used by the provider add/edit dialog.

Suggested starting points:
- The provider add/edit dialog in web-ui (per-agent provider config — Providers tab).
- The backend tRPC endpoint / model-discovery call that fetches the model list from the provider's base URL.
- The shared provider resolution layer (agent-provider-resolver) and base URL handling.

Things to check:
- How the base URL is built/normalized before the fetch (trailing slash, missing scheme, /v1 suffix, port handling). The error string 'Was there a typo in the url or port?' looks like it comes from a fetch/connection failure being surfaced verbatim — find where it's thrown and confirm the actual underlying error (DNS, ECONNREFUSED, wrong path, proxy).
- Whether the runtime's outbound proxy (config/proxy-fetch.ts global fetch monkey-patch) is being applied/bypassed correctly for this request — a misrouted or bypassed proxy could cause a connection failure that surfaces as this message.
- Whether the failure is provider-protocol specific (OpenAI-compatible vs vendor) and whether the right path (e.g. /models) is being requested.

Deliver: a reproduction of the root cause, the fix, and surface a clearer error message to the user distinguishing 'bad URL/port' from 'connection refused / proxy / auth' failures. Add/adjust tests for the model-fetch URL building and error mapping.
